title: The Download: Anthropic launches Claude Science, and California’s carbon manure math
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers yesterday, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a major new product intended to support scientific research…
summary: Anthropic, a prominent AI company, has launched its new flagship product called Claude Science. This major product is specifically designed to support scientific research, as announced at an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers.

title: Finally. $221 million flow into Bitcoin ETFs, ending a painful 10-day outflow streak
content: Spot ETFs had their strongest inflow day in two months, driven by funds other than BlackRock’s IBIT.
summary: Bitcoin spot ETFs recorded a substantial $221 million inflow, marking their strongest day in two months and ending a painful 10-day streak of outflows. This significant positive shift was primarily driven by funds other than BlackRock’s IBIT.

title: A struggling Nasdaq-listed company that tried to copy Saylor's Bitcoin playbook is completely dumping crypto for AI
content: The Nasdaq-listed Korean media company once lined up $1 billion in financing to buy 10,000 bitcoin. A recent filing confirms its balance is now zero as it pivots to AI infrastructure and fights to stay listed.
summary: A struggling Nasdaq-listed Korean media company, which previously attempted to emulate Saylor's Bitcoin strategy by planning a $1 billion acquisition of 10,000 BTC, has now completely divested its crypto holdings. The firm has pivoted entirely to AI infrastructure in an effort to avoid delisting. This marks a significant strategic shift from cryptocurrency to artificial intelligence for the company.

title: Securitize becomes first to debut shares on NYSE and onchain, but it won’t be the last
content: President Brett Redfearn said the firm is in discussions to tokenize other IPOs "definitely ... within the next year.”
summary: Securitize has achieved a significant milestone by becoming the first firm to simultaneously debut shares on both the New York Stock Exchange and onchain, pioneering a hybrid approach to public listings. The company's president, Brett Redfearn, indicated that Securitize is already in discussions to tokenize additional IPOs, with more expected within the next year. This development signals a growing trend towards integrating traditional financial markets with blockchain technology for capital formation.

title: The Download: a startup has a solution for AI’s groupthink problem
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give me a random number between 1…
summary: A new startup is reportedly tackling the 'groupthink problem' observed in Large Language Models (LLMs). This issue causes chatbots like Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini to produce similar or predictable outputs, lacking true diversity. The company aims to develop a solution to help LLMs generate more varied and less homogenous responses.

title: LLMs are stuck in a groupthink groove. This startup is trying to get them out.
content: Let’s start with a game. Open up your chatbot of choice—Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini—and type “Give me a random number between 1 and 10.” You’re going to get 7. Almost always. Now type “Another” and you’ll get 3 or 4. Type “Another” again and you’ll get 8 or 9. That won’t work every time—but if it…
summary: The article highlights that large language models (LLMs) often fall into predictable patterns, described as a 'groupthink groove,' rather than exhibiting true randomness or diverse thought. It demonstrates this by showing LLMs consistently picking the same numbers when asked for a random one. A new startup is introduced with the aim of helping LLMs break free from these repetitive behaviors.

title: Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product
content: At an event for pharmaceutical executives, biotech founders, and researchers on Tuesday, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a major new product intended to support scientific research in the same way that Claude Code supports software engineering. Like Claude Code, Claude Science can autonomously carry out meaningful work when given concise, high-level instructions, and it has access…
summary: Anthropic has launched Claude Science, a new flagship AI product aimed at supporting scientific research in the pharmaceutical and biotech sectors. Similar to Claude Code for software engineering, this product is designed to autonomously carry out meaningful work based on high-level instructions. This marks Anthropic's expansion into specialized AI applications for scientific domains.

title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung
content: The news comes about a week after OpenAI announced its own custom AI chip in a partnership with Broadcom.
summary: Anthropic, a prominent AI company, is reportedly in discussions with Samsung to develop a new custom AI chip. This potential move closely follows OpenAI's recent announcement of its own custom AI chip partnership with Broadcom. These developments signal a growing trend among leading AI firms to design proprietary hardware for optimized AI model performance.

title: OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
content: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI boom.
summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed donating 5% of the company's equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund. This initiative aims to allow the public to share in the financial gains generated by the booming artificial intelligence industry. The proposal revives broader discussions about public participation in the economic benefits of AI development.

title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
content: Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.
summary: Microsoft has launched its own AI deployment company with a substantial $2.5 billion commitment. This move follows similar initiatives by other tech giants like Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic, indicating a growing trend in the direct deployment and scaling of AI technologies by major players. It signifies Microsoft's increased investment and strategic focus on the practical application of artificial intelligence.

title: Venice AI becomes a unicorn with $65M Series A as its privacy-first AI platform takes off
content: Venice AI is already profitable, with annualized run-rate revenues of over $70 million, CEO Erik Voorhees said.
summary: Venice AI, a privacy-first AI platform, has achieved unicorn status after successfully raising a $65 million Series A round. The company, led by CEO Erik Voorhees, is already profitable and boasts annualized run-rate revenues exceeding $70 million.

title: Anthropic’s long-sidelined Fable 5 is greenlit to return
content:
After weeks of negotiating with the Trump administration, Anthropic is finally going to be able to bring Claude Fable 5 back online. In a post on X, Anthropic said it plans to begin restoring access Wednesday to users globally on Claude platforms, and that the company would re-enable access on AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry soon, but with no set timeline.
Anthropic wrote:
We've received notice that the Department of Commerce has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
We'll begin restoring access tomorrow, and will share an update soon.
We're grateful to our users for their patience, and to everyone who work …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Anthropic has announced that its Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models will be brought back online for users globally, starting Wednesday. This follows the Department of Commerce lifting export controls after negotiations with the Trump administration, which had previously sidelined the models. Access will also soon be re-enabled on major cloud platforms including AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry.

title: Ether and solana extend gains as a short squeeze lifts bitcoin toward $62,000
content: Bearish traders lost $281 million in liquidations over 24 hours, nearly double the longs, as bitcoin pushed to its strongest level in two weeks. Ether is up almost 10% on the week and solana nearly 19% while a rebound in tech stocks eased the pressure from the AI trade.
summary: Bitcoin, Ether, and Solana have seen significant gains, with Bitcoin nearing $62,000 due to a short squeeze that resulted in $281 million in liquidations for bearish traders. Ether and Solana have also increased by almost 10% and 19% respectively over the week. This rebound has eased pressure from the AI trade, also lifted by a recovery in tech stocks.

title: Securitize tokenizes $295 million of its own stock on Solana and Avalanche amid NYSE debut
content: The newly-public company's shares are the largest issuer-sponsored tokenized stock at launch, aiming to make a point against rival third-party stock token issuers.
summary: Securitize has tokenized $295 million of its own stock on Solana and Avalanche, marking the largest issuer-sponsored tokenized stock at launch. This move coincides with the company's debut on the NYSE. The tokenization aims to demonstrate the viability of issuer-sponsored tokenized stocks against third-party issuers.

title: EToro invests in onchain derivatives platform Extended as brokers race into DeFi
content: The broker plans to bring perpetual futures into Zengo wallet and expand DeFi products to its core platform as rivals like Robinhood expand onchain offerings.
summary: EToro is investing in Extended, an onchain derivatives platform, to bring perpetual futures to its Zengo wallet and expand DeFi products on its core platform. This move is part of a larger trend of brokers entering the DeFi space, with rivals like Robinhood also expanding their onchain offerings. EToro's investment aims to enhance its offerings and stay competitive in the market.

title: Bitwise says STRC selloff signals crypto cycle nearing a bottom, not Strategy’s breaking point
content: Bitwise said STRC's volatility reflects a late-cycle leverage unwind, with institutions poised to replace Strategy as bitcoin's biggest buyer.
summary: Bitwise believes the recent selloff of STRC signals that the crypto cycle is nearing its bottom, rather than a breaking point for Strategy. According to Bitwise, the volatility of STRC is a result of a late-cycle leverage unwind. Institutions are expected to replace Strategy as the biggest buyer of bitcoin.

title: US Treasury sanctions over 100 ISIS-K crypto addresses that moved over $1.4 million
content: ISIS-K allegedly used its media wing to solicit donations via Tron, Monero, and Bitcoin, highlighting stablecoin issuers' growing role in sanctions enforcement.
summary: The US Treasury has sanctioned over 100 crypto addresses linked to ISIS-K, which allegedly used Tron, Monero, and Bitcoin to solicit donations, moving over $1.4 million. The sanctions highlight the role of stablecoin issuers in enforcing sanctions. This move marks a significant step in regulating crypto transactions related to terrorist activities.

title: Ondo Finance debuts SEC-aligned tokenized stock model with BlackRock ETF, Micron shares
content: Broadridge and transfer agent Oasis Pro underpin a new structure that keeps tokenized securities within existing U.S. market rules, based on the SEC's third-party custodial model.
summary: Ondo Finance has introduced a tokenized stock model that aligns with SEC guidelines, utilizing a third-party custodial model and partnering with Broadridge and Oasis Pro. This model allows tokenized securities to operate within existing US market rules. The debut features a BlackRock ETF and Micron shares.

title: Three years after MiCA became law, Europe's crypto framework is undergoing a rethink
content: Europe’s MiCA regime is now up for review, known colloquially as “MiCA 2.0,” through a consultation that closes around September.
summary: The European crypto framework, known as MiCA, is undergoing a review process, dubbed 'MiCA 2.0', with a consultation period set to close in September. This review comes three years after MiCA became law, indicating a potential shift in Europe's crypto regulatory landscape. The consultation aims to gather feedback and reassess the current framework.

title: Warsh's comments set the stage for U.S. jobs data to ignite bitcoin, gold rally
content: Your day-ahead look for July 2, 2026
summary: Federal Reserve Governor Warsh's comments may impact the US jobs data, potentially influencing a rally in bitcoin and gold prices. This development is significant as it connects economic indicators to cryptocurrency market fluctuations. The upcoming jobs data release is anticipated to ignite a notable reaction in these markets.

title: ChatGPT developer OpenAI said to discuss offering U.S. government a 5% stake: FT
content: The proposal aims to give the American public a financial stake in AI while easing political scrutiny of the industry.
summary: OpenAI, the developer of ChatGPT, is reportedly in talks to offer the US government a 5% stake, aiming to provide the public with a financial interest in AI and reduce political scrutiny. This move could have significant implications for the AI industry and its relationship with the government. The proposal is seen as a way to increase transparency and trust in AI development.

title: Live updates: Bitcoin holds above $61,000 as momentum stocks plunge to start quarter
summary: Bitcoin's price remains above $61,000 despite a decline in momentum stocks at the start of the quarter. This stability suggests a potential disconnect between the cryptocurrency market and traditional stocks. The situation is being closely monitored for its implications on the broader financial market.

title: Metaplanet buys another $170 million of bitcoin expanding treasury to 43,000 BTC
content: The Japanese firm, now the world's third largest publicly traded bitcoin holder, reported stronger Bitcoin Income Generation revenue.
summary: Metaplanet, a Japanese firm, has purchased an additional $170 million of bitcoin, expanding its treasury to 43,000 BTC, and reported increased revenue from Bitcoin Income Generation. This move solidifies the company's position as the world's third largest publicly traded bitcoin holder. The investment underscores the firm's confidence in the cryptocurrency's potential.

title: Taiko fully restores cross-chain bridge just 10 days after a $1.7 million hack
content: The protocol completed a multi-stage recovery that included an independent security review. The TAIKO token has surged as much as 136% in recent trading.
summary: The Taiko protocol has restored its cross-chain bridge just 10 days after a $1.7 million hack, completing a multi-stage recovery process that included an independent security review. The TAIKO token has seen a significant surge in value, increasing by as much as 136% in recent trading. This rapid recovery is a positive sign for the protocol's security and resilience.

title: Solana launches onchain governance and sets entry fee at 100,000 SOL staked
content: Validators and the people who stake with them can now formally signal where the network should go through a new system called Solana Governance Proposals. Any validator with 100,000 SOL behind it can open one, and stakers can overrule how their validator votes.
summary: Solana has introduced on-chain governance, allowing validators and stakers to participate in decision-making through Solana Governance Proposals. Validators with 100,000 SOL can create proposals, and stakers can override their validator's votes. This move aims to increase community involvement in the network's development.

title: Bitcoin's long-term holders have returned to accumulation
content: Long-term holding wallets have shifted to net accumulation from net distribution, according to Glassnode.
summary: Bitcoin's long-term holders are now accumulating the cryptocurrency again, marking a shift from net distribution, according to data from Glassnode. This change in behavior could indicate a bullish trend. The return to accumulation may signal a positive outlook for the cryptocurrency's future.

title: Securitize gains on NYSE debut with tokenized stocks live on Solana, Avalanche
content: Securitize has issued tokenized versions of its shares on Solana and Avalanche, marking the first time a newly public company has done so on its debut.
summary: Securitize has made its debut on the NYSE and has issued tokenized versions of its shares on Solana and Avalanche, a first for a newly public company. This move allows for greater accessibility and liquidity for investors. The company's innovative approach to traditional stock offerings has generated significant interest.

title: Bitcoin holds $61K after US jobs data report, AI sector weakness: Did BTC bottom?
content: Bitcoin bulls may make a run on $70,000 after weak US jobs data eased rate hike fears and capital looks to rotate into BTC and gold.
summary: Bitcoin's price held at $61K after the release of US jobs data, which eased fears of a rate hike, potentially leading to increased investment in BTC and gold. This development may trigger a bull run, targeting $70,000. The weak US jobs data and AI sector weakness are key factors in this trend.

title: IMF says tokenization could transform settlement and financial stability
content: The global lender says blockchain-based finance could streamline markets but warns that fragmented standards and regulations may create new systemic risks.
summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) believes tokenization has the potential to transform settlement and financial stability through blockchain-based finance, but notes that fragmented standards and regulations may pose new risks. This could streamline markets and improve efficiency. However, regulatory challenges need to be addressed.

title: Russia on track for digital ruble rollout on Sept. 1: Central bank governor
content: EU authorities already preemptively sanctioned Russia’s digital ruble in 2025 as part of measures in response to the country’s war against Ukraine.
summary: Russia is set to roll out its digital ruble on September 1, according to the central bank governor. The EU has already imposed sanctions on the digital ruble in 2025 due to Russia's war against Ukraine. This development may impact global digital currency regulations.

title: Bitcoin price taps new July high above $62K on weak US jobs data
content: Bitcoin took daily gains to nearly 4% on the second day of "green July" as US labor-market signals supported an easing inflation policy from the Federal Reserve.
summary: Bitcoin's price has reached a new high above $62K in July, with daily gains of nearly 4%, following the release of weak US jobs data. This development may lead to an easing inflation policy from the Federal Reserve. The news is positive for Bitcoin, indicating a potential bullish trend.

title: Ondo expands tokenized equities with onchain shareholder voting
content: Ondo's latest partnership adds shareholder governance to tokenized stocks as competition in blockchain-based equity offerings accelerates.
summary: Ondo has partnered to introduce on-chain shareholder voting for tokenized equities, enhancing governance in blockchain-based stock offerings. This move reflects increasing competition in the space. The development aims to improve the overall experience of tokenized equity holders.

title: The biggest blockchain upgrades still to come in 2026
content: From Ethereum’s Glamsterdam to Solana’s Alpenglow, 2026’s most important developments are protocol upgrades, not price charts.
summary: The article discusses the biggest blockchain upgrades expected in 2026, focusing on protocol upgrades rather than price charts. Ethereum's Glamsterdam and Solana's Alpenglow are mentioned as significant developments. These upgrades are expected to have a notable impact on the blockchain community.

title: UK payments blueprint outlines tokenized payments for ‘multi-money ecosystem’
content: UK regulators published an update to a national retail payments blueprint, calling for infrastructure support for tokenization and interoperability with new forms of digital money.
summary: UK regulators have published an updated national retail payments blueprint, which Includes support for tokenized payments and interoperability with new forms of digital money, aiming for a 'multi-money ecosystem'. The update outlines infrastructure requirements for this ecosystem. This development may significantly impact the UK's payment landscape.

title: Standard Chartered, Circle bring USDC minting onto banking rails
content: Standard Chartered and Circle launch bank-led USDC minting and redemption for institutions, starting in Dubai’s DIFC with planned global expansion.
summary: Standard Chartered and Circle have launched a bank-led USDC minting and redemption service for institutions, starting in Dubai's DIFC, with plans for global expansion. This service allows for the direct minting and redemption of USDC on banking rails, streamlining the process for institutional clients. The partnership aims to increase the adoption of USDC globally.

title: Solana Foundation launches framework for protocol-level governance
content: The Solana Foundation launched a framework for protocol-level governance, enabling validators with at least 100,000 delegated SOL to publish new proposals.
summary: The Solana Foundation has launched a framework for protocol-level governance, allowing validators with a significant amount of delegated SOL to publish new proposals. This move enables more decentralized decision-making within the Solana network. The framework requires validators to have at least 100,000 delegated SOL to participate.

title: OFAC sanctions 134 ISIS-K crypto wallet addresses as Tether freezes funds
content: OFAC added 134 crypto wallet addresses linked to ISIS-K to its sanctions list, including 131 Tron addresses that received more than $1.4 million in crypto donations, according to Chainalysis.
summary: The US Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) has sanctioned 134 crypto wallet addresses linked to ISIS-K, with Tether freezing the associated funds. The majority of the addresses, 131, are on the Tron network and received over $1.4 million in cryptocurrency donations. This action aims to disrupt ISIS-K's ability to fund its activities through cryptocurrency.

title: Metaplanet buys 2,823 BTC, surpasses 43,000 in Bitcoin holdings
content: Metaplanet bought 2,823 Bitcoin during the second quarter, reducing its average acquisition cost to $106,500 per BTC, and reported $10.9 million in revenue from its income generation strategy.
summary: Metaplanet has increased its Bitcoin holdings to over 43,000 by purchasing 2,823 BTC in the second quarter, bringing its average acquisition cost down to $106,500 per BTC. The company also reported $10.9 million in revenue from its income generation strategy. This significant investment underscores Metaplanet's confidence in Bitcoin's potential.

title: OpenAI weighs 5% stake for US government amid Trump talks: FT
content: OpenAI has reportedly discussed giving the US government a 5% equity stake in early Trump administration talks as Washington tightens AI model oversight.
summary: OpenAI has discussed giving the US government a 5% equity stake in the company as part of early talks with the Trump administration. The move comes as Washington tightens its oversight of AI models. This development may have significant implications for the AI community.

title: Venice AI becomes unicorn after $65M Series A as AI privacy concerns grow
content: The funding round is Venice AI’s first external capital raise since it launched in May 2024.
summary: Venice AI has raised $65M in Series A funding, becoming a unicorn, amidst growing concerns over AI privacy. This funding round is the company's first external capital raise since its launch in May 2024. The investment highlights the increasing interest in AI companies despite privacy concerns.

title: Ondo tokenizes BlackRock’s IVV ETF and Micron stock under US custodial model
content: Ondo Finance launches tokenized BlackRock IVV ETF and Micron shares under SEC-defined custodial model with Ethereum-based settlement.
summary: Ondo Finance has launched tokenized versions of BlackRock's IVV ETF and Micron stock, using a US custodial model with Ethereum-based settlement. This allows for the representation of traditional assets on the blockchain. The launch is under a model defined by the SEC.

title: ‘Accumulation beneath the surface’: Bitcoin rebounds above $61,000 as long-term holders accumulate amid steady ETF outflows
content: Bitcoin reclaims $61,000 as Glassnode and Bitfinex flag long-term holder accumulation beneath persistent ETF selling.
summary: Bitcoin has reclaimed the $61,000 mark following a rebound in its price. This increase is occurring despite ongoing ETF selling, as analytics firms Glassnode and Bitfinex report significant accumulation by long-term holders.

title: Avalanche Treasury stock plunges 73% since debut as company’s AVAX holdings lose value
content: At the end of the first quarter, the company's management raised 'substantial doubt' about its ability to continue operations.
summary: Avalanche Treasury's stock has plummeted 73% since its debut, largely due to the declining value of the company's AVAX holdings. The company's management has expressed significant concerns about its ability to continue operations, citing substantial doubt. This development may have significant implications for the Avalanche ecosystem and the broader crypto market.

title: Preferred Stock Is Becoming Bitcoin Treasury Firms’ Financing Tool of Choice: Report
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Preferred Stock Is Becoming Bitcoin Treasury Firms’ Financing Tool of Choice: Report
Bitcoin-backed preferred shares — led by Strategy and emerging players like Strive—have grown into a roughly $13B market by offering high-yield.
This post Preferred Stock Is Becoming Bitcoin Treasury Firms’ Financing Tool of Choice: Report first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Bitcoin treasury firms are increasingly using preferred stock as a financing tool, with a market size of approximately $13B, offering high-yield Bitcoin-backed shares. This trend is led by companies like Strategy and Strive. The growth of this market indicates a shift in financing strategies for Bitcoin firms.

title: Teaching AI to run with the turbines
content: Artificial intelligence may have captured the public imagination through chatbots and image generators, but some of its most consequential use cases are unfolding far from consumer-facing tools. In industries where physical infrastructure, operational continuity, and safety are paramount, AI is becoming a core operating layer. With its sprawling industrial systems and constant stream of operational…
summary: The article highlights the impactful applications of artificial intelligence beyond consumer tools, focusing on its role as a core operating layer in critical industrial sectors. AI is being utilized to manage complex physical infrastructure, ensure operational continuity, and enhance safety, specifically through its deployment with turbines and vast industrial systems.

title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
content: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app that lets users generate and share interactive mini games using text prompts.
summary: Meta has quietly launched Pocket, an experimental AI app designed for generating and sharing interactive mini-games. Users can create these "vibe-coded" games using simple text prompts. This represents a new application of AI in user-generated content for gaming.

title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
content: Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office and Google Apps with AI.
summary: Indian tech tycoon Bhavin Turakhia has invested $30 million of his own money into Neo, his fifth venture. Neo aims to be an AI-powered alternative to established enterprise software such as Microsoft Office and Google Apps. This initiative marks his latest effort to disrupt the productivity software market using artificial intelligence.

title: Cloudflare’s new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers’ content
content: Cloudflare is giving AI companies until September 15 to separate web crawlers used for search from those used for AI training and agents, or risk being blocked by default on many publisher sites.
summary: Cloudflare has introduced a new policy requiring AI companies to pay for publishers' content, giving them until September 15 to comply. The policy aims to separate web crawlers used for search from those used for AI training, with non-compliant companies risking being blocked by default. This move affects how AI companies access and utilize online content.

title: Meta, like SpaceX, looks to turn excess AI compute into cash
content: Meta is developing plans for a cloud infrastructure business, selling access to AI compute power and models. The move would pit it against the big cloud providers like Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.
summary: Meta is reportedly planning to launch a cloud infrastructure business, aiming to monetize its excess AI compute power and models. This strategic move would position Meta as a direct competitor against established cloud providers such as Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.

title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
content: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based AI lab founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, is now valued at more than $500 million.
summary: EquiLibre Technologies, founded by three former DeepMind researchers, has reached a valuation of over $500 million. The company is using AI to generate profits for quant hedge funds. This achievement demonstrates the successful application of AI in finance.

title: Google’s NotebookLM can sum up your research in a TikTok-style clip
content:
Google's NotebookLM is adding a new way to catch up on your notes: TikTok-style AI videos. The new feature is rolling out to Google AI Ultra and Pro subscribers, allowing NotebookLM to generate 60-second vertical AI clips based on the sources you upload to the app.
The example shared by Google details Australia's unsuccessful war on emus, pairing paper cutout-style AI art of emus with narration. It adds to some of the other ways NotebookLM lets you interact with your research, including by generating AI podcasts, cinematic videos, and visual explainers.


Doom scrolling but make it educational ðŸ¤“
Introducing Short Video Overviews in Notebo …


Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Google's NotebookLM is introducing a new feature for its AI Ultra and Pro subscribers, allowing the generation of 60-second TikTok-style AI video summaries from uploaded research sources. This expands the platform's AI-driven content creation capabilities, which already include AI podcasts and cinematic videos. The aim is to provide quick, educational overviews of research material.

title: Tidal won’t pay royalties on AI-generated music, but isn’t banning it outright
content:
Tidal shared its new policies regarding AI-generated music today and how the platform plans to "protect artists" and "inform listeners." Instead of banning it outright, starting on July 15th Tidal will label tracks it has identified as being 100 percent AI-generated with an icon. But starting today those tracks will no longer be monetizable. "Tidal's priority is ensuring royalties go to original works directly produced, written, and performed by people. We will therefore not knowingly attribute royalties to music we identify as wholly AI-generated," the company's announcement reads. 
The platform didn't specify what tools it's using to iden …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Tidal has introduced a new policy for AI-generated music, labeling tracks that are 100% AI-generated with an icon and making them non-monetizable, while prioritizing royalties for original works created by humans. This change will take effect on July 15th. The platform aims to protect artists and inform listeners.

title: Musk’s X poses “serious risk to Americans’ privacy,” advocates warn FTC
content: FTC urged to reject Elon Musk’s bid to end X monitoring amid AI concerns.
summary: The FTC is being urged to reject Elon Musk's bid to end X monitoring due to AI concerns, with advocates warning of serious risks to Americans' privacy. Elon Musk's X is posing significant concerns, prompting calls for regulatory action. The situation highlights growing tensions between tech innovation and user privacy.

title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
content: Telling an LLM that 2 + 2 = 5 is enough to make it follow forbidden instructions.
summary: A new attack has been discovered that can manipulate large language models (LLMs) into following forbidden instructions by providing false information, such as stating that 2 + 2 = 5. This vulnerability highlights the risks of AI-powered browsers. The attack demonstrates the potential for LLMs to be misled and compromised.

title: South Korea to spend $1T on more memory chip production and humanoid robots
content: South Korea targets physical AI lead and commercial humanoid robots by 2028.
summary: South Korea plans to invest $1 trillion to boost its memory chip production and develop humanoid robots, aiming to take the lead in physical AI and commercialize humanoid robots by 2028. This move is expected to strengthen the country's position in the tech industry. The investment will likely have a significant impact on the global AI and robotics market.

title: Notion killing Skiff-influenced email app since most users use AI agents instead
content: Notion is "going all in on using agents to run your inbox."
summary: Notion is discontinuing its Skiff-influenced email app, citing that most users prefer using AI agents to manage their inbox instead. This move indicates a shift towards AI-powered tools for email management. Notion will focus on using agents to run inboxes.

title: IBM claims world’s first sub-1 nanometer chip technology
content: IBM’s nanostack transistors could boost chip performance or energy efficiency.
summary: IBM has announced a breakthrough in chip technology, developing the world's first sub-1 nanometer chip using nanostack transistors, which could lead to significant improvements in performance or energy efficiency. This innovation has the potential to revolutionize the tech industry. IBM's achievement is a major milestone in semiconductor research.

title: Aave brings V3 lending and GHO stablecoin to Monad
content: Aave launched its V3 lending protocol on Monad with 12 supported assets as the network commits $15 million in first-year incentives to build liquidity and adoption.
summary: Aave has launched its V3 lending protocol on Monad, supported by 12 assets, and will provide $15 million in incentives over the first year. This move aims to increase liquidity and adoption on the network. The launch also includes the introduction of the GHO stablecoin.

title: eToro leads $12.5 million round in onchain perps exchange Extended
content: "The partnership will focus on expanding access to global financial markets through next-generation on-chain infrastructure," eToro said.
summary: eToro has led a $12.5 million investment round in Extended, an on-chain perpetual exchange, to expand access to global financial markets. The partnership aims to utilize next-generation on-chain infrastructure. This investment highlights eToro's interest in decentralized finance and on-chain trading solutions.

title: FBI Director Kash Patel Did Not Disclose Six-Figure Strategy (MSTR) Stake: Report
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

FBI Director Kash Patel Did Not Disclose Six-Figure Strategy (MSTR) Stake: Report
The late disclosure of FBI Director Kash Patel's six-figure investment in Strategy has reignited some scrutiny over ethics compliance.
This post FBI Director Kash Patel Did Not Disclose Six-Figure Strategy (MSTR) Stake: Report first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: FBI Director Kash Patel reportedly failed to disclose a significant six-figure investment in Strategy (MSTR), a company known for its substantial Bitcoin holdings. This late disclosure has reignited scrutiny over ethical compliance for high-ranking government officials.

title: The Download: AI “coworkers” and stratospheric internet
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. AI agents are not your “coworkers” Imagine coming in to work to learn that a new underling will report to you. The worker is not a person but an AI tool—one…
summary: This edition of The Download newsletter discusses the integration of AI agents into the workplace. It specifically explores the idea of AI tools serving as "underlings" reporting to human employees, rather than being considered true "coworkers." The article highlights the evolving dynamics of human-AI collaboration and the changing nature of work.

title: Agriculture is ready for AI, but its data isn’t
content: Artificial intelligence is transforming what is possible in agriculture, but industry leaders should be wary of investing in AI without first laying the groundwork.  The use cases are promising, especially for an industry navigating volatile fertilizer costs, unpredictable weather, and margins that leave little room for error. Research shows AI-enabled predictive models can improve crop…
summary: The article highlights the transformative potential of AI in agriculture, especially for tackling challenges like volatile costs and unpredictable weather. However, it cautions industry leaders against investing in AI without first laying the necessary data groundwork. Research indicates that AI-enabled predictive models can significantly improve crop outcomes.

title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
content: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.
summary: Mark Zuckerberg, CEO of Meta, reportedly informed staff at an internal meeting that the development of AI agents has not progressed as quickly as he had hoped. He indicated that the company's AI development efforts were moving slower than anticipated. This suggests some internal challenges or revised timelines for advanced AI projects at Meta.

title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
content: Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.
summary: Ben Guez has developed an automated script that uses OpenClaw and Claude AI code, integrated with Instagram, to connect with potential international partners for dating. This system has reportedly led to him having a "bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs."

title: SpaceX has an AI device prototype, and it sure sounds phone-ish
content: SpaceX reportedly showed investors a "handset-like" AI device before going public. It could be another signal SpaceX wants to expand into wireless.
summary: SpaceX has reportedly showcased a "handset-like" AI device prototype to investors before going public. This development signals a potential strategic move by SpaceX to broaden its operations into the wireless communication market.

title: The ‘Father of the Internet’ is finally retiring
content: Vinton Cerf, one of the creators of the protocols underlying the internet, will step down as Google's chief internet evangelist next week.
summary: Vinton Cerf, widely recognized as one of the key figures behind the creation of the internet's foundational protocols, is set to retire. He will be stepping down next week from his long-standing position as Google's chief internet evangelist.

title: XRP bulls test path back toward $1.10 as token zips 4% higher
content: Buyers defended a higher low and pushed XRP toward near-term resistance, though the move still needs a clean break above $1.10 to confirm momentum.
summary: XRP's price has increased by 4% as buyers push towards near-term resistance, testing the path back to $1.10. The momentum still needs confirmation with a clean break above $1.10. This move may indicate a potential trend reversal for XRP.

title: SBI Crypto to shut down mining pool that holds roughly 2% of Bitcoin's hashrate
content: Miners must redirect their hashrate before the July 31 cutoff, as the pool will stop accepting contributions after that date.
summary: SBI Crypto is shutting down its Bitcoin mining pool, which accounts for around 2% of the cryptocurrency's hashrate, with miners required to redirect their hashrate by July 31. The pool will stop accepting contributions after this date. This change may impact Bitcoin's mining landscape.

title: JPMorgan says Strategy's bitcoin sales policy adds 'two-way risk' to crypto markets
content: The bank said Strategy's bitcoin sales policy adds avoidable market uncertainty and should be replaced with equity issuance to build cash reserves.
summary: JPMorgan has criticized Strategy's bitcoin sales policy, stating it adds uncertainty to crypto markets. The bank suggests replacing this policy with equity issuance to build cash reserves, reducing market risk. This commentary may impact bitcoin prices and market stability.

title: U.S. payroll growth slowed sharply in June, with only 57,000 jobs added
content: This morning's data could slow market expectations for a Fed rate hike as soon as this summer or early Fall.
summary: The U.S. payroll growth slowed down in June with only 57,000 jobs added, which may impact market expectations. This could potentially delay a Federal Reserve rate hike planned for summer or early fall. The slower job growth may have significant implications for the economy.

title: Smaller tokens lead as bitcoin, sol rally in 'first real bounce of the selloff'
content: Bitcoin and major cryptocurrencies rebounded on dovish Federal Reserve signals, with speculative tokens like Memecore’s M and Audiera’s BEAT leading gains.
summary: Bitcoin and major cryptocurrencies rebounded due to dovish Federal Reserve signals, with smaller tokens like M and BEAT leading the gains. This rebound is considered the first real bounce of the selloff. The rally indicates a potential shift in market sentiment.

title: Bitcoin rises 4% to above $61,000 as inflation fears soften
content: Bitcoin rose after Fed Chair Kevin Warsh said inflation risks had eased. A 7.9% drop in South Korea's Kospi on renewed AI chip worries did little to dent the bid.
summary: Bitcoin's price increased by 4% to over $61,000 due to easing inflation fears, as stated by Fed Chair Kevin Warsh. This rise occurred despite a 7.9% drop in South Korea's Kospi index, which was affected by AI chip concerns. The increase in Bitcoin's price suggests a positive market sentiment.

title: XRP edges higher as whale activity rises while retail traders stay cautious
content: New wallet creation hit a three-month high and large-holder activity strengthened, but XRP still needs to reclaim $1.10 before the recovery looks convincing.
summary: XRP's price has increased due to rising whale activity, with new wallet creation reaching a three-month high and large-holder activity strengthening. However, a recovery is not yet convincing as XRP needs to reclaim $1.10. Retail traders remain cautious, awaiting further price movement.

title: FBI Director Kash Patel caught sleeping on required disclosure of six-figure MSTR investment: Report
content: Patel loaded up on BTC-holder MSTR shares but failed to file a timely disclosure, according to a report by nonpartisan news outlet NOTUS.
summary: FBI Director Kash Patel failed to disclose a six-figure investment in MSTR, a company that holds BTC, in a timely manner, according to a report. This raises concerns about potential conflicts of interest. The investment was not properly reported, prompting questions about transparency.

title: Ether, solana, dogecoin in the green after Warsh comments push bitcoin above $60,000
content: Bitcoin climbed back above $60,000 for the first time in over a week after Fed Chair Kevin Warsh said inflation risks had come down. Solana led the majors, up about 16% on the week, while a semiconductor selloff hit Asian markets and dented the AI trade that has pulled capital from crypto all quarter.
summary: Bitcoin surpassed $60,000 after Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's comments on reduced inflation risks, with other cryptocurrencies like Solana and Dogecoin also seeing gains. Solana led the majors with a 16% increase, despite a semiconductor selloff in Asian markets. The crypto market has been impacted by an AI trade that has pulled capital away from it this quarter.

title: AI agent development hasn’t accelerated as expected, Zuckerberg says
content: Mark Zuckerberg’s comments came on the same day that Meta expanded its Meta Business Agent globally for businesses on Instagram, Messenger and WhatsApp.
summary: Mark Zuckerberg stated that AI agent development has not accelerated as expected, despite Meta expanding its Meta Business Agent globally for businesses on Instagram, Messenger, and WhatsApp. This announcement came on the same day as the expansion. The news may impact Meta's business and AI development strategies.

title: US dominates Polymarket political bets despite geoblock: Report
content: New data from Allium suggest that US users are bypassing geoblocks to bet on markets offered on Polymarket’s global platform.
summary: US users are finding ways to bypass geoblocks to participate in political bets on Polymarket, a global platform, according to new data from Allium. This suggests that interest in the platform's markets is high despite restrictions. The data highlights the determination of US users to access the platform.

title: Strategy will be ‘less important’ in Bitcoin after STRC incident: Bitwise
content: Bitwise’s Matt Hougan said Strategy's STRC offer of high yields and low volatility was always a questionable fit for buying Bitcoin, as the cryptocurrency offers neither.
summary: Matt Hougan from Bitwise stated that strategy will be less important in Bitcoin following the STRC incident, as Bitcoin does not offer high yields and low volatility. The STRC offer was seen as a questionable fit for buying Bitcoin. This shift may impact investment approaches in the crypto market.

title: Is Bitcoin heading for $65K? Sharplink buys $16M ETH: Market Moves
content: Analysts are divided on Bitcoin's next move, but there's some hope at last. Meanwhile, SharpLink has resumed buying Ether after an eight-week pause, and other market news.
summary: Analysts are uncertain about Bitcoin's next move, but there is some optimism, with potential targets including $65K. Meanwhile, SharpLink has resumed buying Ether after an eight-week pause. The crypto market is showing signs of activity with various other market moves.

title: Ripple co-founder backs venture launched by US senator's son: Report
content: While engaged in negotiations over ethics in a crypto market structure bill in Congress, Senator Kirsten Gillibrand said she had “no involvement” in her son’s derivatives exchange, backed by at least one industry figure.
summary: Ripple co-founder has backed a venture launched by the son of US Senator Kirsten Gillibrand, who claims to have no involvement in the project despite being engaged in crypto market structure bill negotiations. The venture is a derivatives exchange and has received backing from at least one industry figure. This development raises questions about potential conflicts of interest.

title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
content: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.
summary: The article provides an overview of the latest news and trends in the crypto space, covering Bitcoin, blockchain, DeFi, Web3, and crypto regulation. It aims to keep readers updated on daily events impacting the crypto market. The article serves as a daily briefing for those interested in crypto news.

title: FBI director discloses Strategy holdings months after deadline: Report
content: Strategy is a registered US government contractor, though Kash Patel claimed that “no current conflict exists” with his $100,001 and $250,000 worth of the company’s stock that he reported late.
summary: FBI director Kash Patel has disclosed his holdings in Strategy, a US government contractor, months after the deadline, reporting stock worth between $100,001 and $250,000. Patel claimed there was no conflict of interest despite the late disclosure. The report raises questions about potential conflicts of interest in government contracting.

title: SBI Crypto shuts Bitcoin mining pool after 5-year run
content: SBI Crypto will shut down its Bitcoin mining pool on July 31 after more than five years, ranking 12th globally with about 2.2% of hashrate share.
summary: SBI Crypto is shutting down its Bitcoin mining pool on July 31 after a 5-year run, having ranked 12th globally with a 2.2% hashrate share. The closure marks the end of an era for the mining pool. This decision may impact the Bitcoin network's hashrate distribution.

title: Yield-bearing stablecoin slowdown ends 3-year run for crypto-native products
content: Yield-bearing stablecoin supply fell 15% in Q2 as sUSDe and sUSDS contracted, while Treasury-backed products including BUIDL, USYC and USDY continued to grow.
summary: The supply of yield-bearing stablecoins has decreased by 15% in Q2, marking an end to a 3-year growth trend for crypto-native products, while Treasury-backed products continue to grow. This shift may indicate a change in investor preferences. The contraction includes stablecoins such as sUSDe and sUSDS.

title: Taiko reopens bridge after $1.7M exploit, says users made whole
content: Taiko restored bridge transfers after replenishing asset backing and completing security fixes following an 11-day network disruption.
summary: Taiko has reopened its bridge after resolving a $1.7M exploit, ensuring users' funds are secure. The network was disrupted for 11 days while Taiko replenished asset backing and implemented security fixes. Users' assets have been restored, and bridge transfers have resumed.

title: France to strengthen response as crypto wrench attacks hit 77
content: French Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez promised a “more ambitious” three-part plan to reinforce security measures for the crypto sector.
summary: France's Interior Minister has announced a plan to strengthen security measures for the crypto sector in response to a series of crypto wrench attacks, which have hit 77 targets. The plan aims to reinforce security and combat these attacks. The move is seen as a response to the growing threat of crypto-related crimes.

title: Irish authorities recover another 500 bitcoins from lost Clifton Collins drug stash
content: Onchain data from Arkham Intelligence now shows wallets tagged "Clifton Collins" holding roughly 4,500 BTC.
summary: Irish authorities have recovered an additional 500 bitcoins from a lost drug stash linked to Clifton Collins, bringing the total to roughly 4,500 BTC. The recovery was made possible through onchain data analysis by Arkham Intelligence. This seizure is a significant development in the case.

title: Anchorage Digital adds Lido support, giving institutions access to wstETH
content: Through the integration, institutional clients gain direct access to Lido's wrapped staked ETH token, wstETH, via Anchorage’s platform.
summary: Anchorage Digital has integrated Lido, allowing institutional clients to access wrapped staked ETH token, wstETH, directly through its platform. This integration provides institutions with a new opportunity to engage with the Ethereum ecosystem. The move is expected to increase institutional participation in crypto staking.

title: Trump-backed American Bitcoin sets 1-for-15 reverse stock split in bid to maintain Nasdaq listing
content: American Bitcoin's reverse split will reduce outstanding shares from roughly 1.09 billion to about 73 million.
summary: American Bitcoin is undergoing a 1-for-15 reverse stock split to reduce its outstanding shares from 1.09 billion to 73 million in an effort to maintain its Nasdaq listing. This move is aimed at increasing the company's stock price. The company is backed by former US President Donald Trump.

title: The 2036 Issue: The Future Is Now, Words of Wisdom from Jeff Booth
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

The 2036 Issue: The Future Is Now, Words of Wisdom from Jeff Booth
From The 2036 Issue: An interview with Jeff Booth on where Bitcoin is going over the next decade, by Frank Corva. 
This post The 2036 Issue: The Future Is Now, Words of Wisdom from Jeff Booth first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Frank Corva.
summary: Jeff Booth shared his thoughts on the future of Bitcoin in an interview with Frank Corva, discussing where the cryptocurrency is headed over the next decade. Booth's insights provide valuable perspective on the potential growth and development of Bitcoin. The interview is part of The 2036 Issue, a collection of articles exploring the future of Bitcoin.

title: Jersey Mike’s IPO illustrates how bad the AI hype has become
content: Just for kicks, I took a look at Jersey Mike's IPO documents. Surely a sandwich shop would have no need to mention AI. But lo and behold.
summary: The article uses Jersey Mike's IPO documents to illustrate the current pervasive AI hype, noting that even a sandwich shop surprisingly included mentions of AI. The author points to this unexpected finding as evidence of how excessively AI is being incorporated into business narratives.

title: Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic’s Mythos and Fable models
content: The Trump administration's erratic approach to AI policymaking has left companies across the industry with little clarity about what will govern future model releases.
summary: The Trump administration has dropped restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models, amidst an erratic approach to AI policymaking. This move leaves companies with little clarity on future model releases. The decision may impact the development and release of AI models.

title: How to burst the AI bubble: Strike at its roots
content: Sci-fi author/tech journalist Cory Doctorow on his new book, The Reverse Centaur's Guide to Life After AI.
summary: Sci-fi author and tech journalist Cory Doctorow has written a new book, The Reverse Centaur's Guide to Life After AI, which explores the concept of AI and its potential impact on society. Doctorow's work aims to provide a guide for navigating a world with AI. The book offers a unique perspective on the AI phenomenon.

title: Robinhood links with dYdX Labs to launch new DEX Arcus

title: Dubai crypto market hits 50 licensed firms after new VARA approval

title: Trumps’ American Bitcoin sinks 8.4% ahead of reverse stock split to stay listed
content: The Trump sons' American Bitcoin hit a low on Wednesday ahead of the company’s reverse stock split, which aims to buoy shares and keep it on the Nasdaq.

title: Institutional Bitcoin Adoption Explained: How Blackrock, Fidelity and Others Embraced BTC
content: Institutional adoption is when organizations such as asset managers, corporations, hedge funds, banks, pension funds, and insurers get involved in crypto. This can mean investing in bitcoin directly or building products and services around it. The trend accelerated sharply after spot bitcoin ETFs launched in January 2024 and now spans most categories of regulated institutional […]

title: CFTC’s Selig says Illinois lawmakers ‘decided they know better’ on crypto tax
content: CFTC Chair Michael Selig sharply criticized Illinois' decision to pass a law that puts into place a 0.2% tax on crypto transactions.

title: JPMorgan says Strategy’s bitcoin sale policy introduced ‘avoidable’ risk into crypto markets
content: Strategy introduced "avoidable two-way risk" into crypto markets with its recent bitcoin sale policy, according to JPMorgan analysts.

content: Vlad Tenev spoke to his belief in the merging of TradFi and crypto when asked if digital assets have entered an "enduring" downturn.

title: Bitget expands Stock+ platform with US stock options trading
content: Bitget launched U.S. stock options, adding equity derivatives to its Stock+ platform alongside crypto and tokenized stocks.

title: Peter Thiel-backed crypto-friendly Erebor Bank eyes $8 billion valuation as deposits nearly quadruple: Bloomberg
content: Erebor Bank, backed by Palmer Luckey and Peter Thiel, is in talks to raise funding at a valuation of at least $8 billion, per Bloomberg.

title: ‘I think we’re nearing the bottom’: Bitwise CIO says Strategy’s STRC selloff is part of bitcoin’s end-of-cycle dynamics
content: Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan says Strategy's STRC selloff reflects the kind of "end-of-cycle" deleveraging that precedes a bitcoin market bottom.

content: FBI Director Kash Patel disclosed a six-figure Strategy stock purchase months after execution in a delayed federal ethics filing.

title: Metaplanet adds 2,823 bitcoin to holdings in Q2, bringing total to 43,000 BTC
content: Metaplanet spent $222 million on the bitcoin purchase during the second quarter, translating to $78,608 per bitcoin.

title: Kalshi and Polymarket’s combined volume surges 75% to $45 billion in June amid World Cup fever
content: Among the three, Kalshi saw the largest month-over-month increase in volume, growing 87.4% to $31.5 billion from $16.81 billion.

title: Ark Invest scoops up $18 million in Circle shares as stock dips 41% over past month
content: Circle dropped 1% today after falling nearly 18% on Tuesday following the launch of the rival stablecoin project OUSD.

title: Bitcoin Exchange Inflows Spike to 49,000 BTC in a Day, Signaling More Volatility is Coming: Report
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Exchange Inflows Spike to 49,000 BTC in a Day, Signaling More Volatility is Coming: Report
CryptoQuant reported Bitcoin exchange inflows surged to 49,000 BTC on June 30 — a rare spike historically linked to heightened volatility and often downside risk — even as Bitcoin rebounded above the key $60,000 support level.
This post Bitcoin Exchange Inflows Spike to 49,000 BTC in a Day, Signaling More Volatility is Coming: Report first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

title: Wavespace Launches MiCA-Compliant Self-Custodial Bitcoin Debit Card Powered by Lightning and NWC
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Wavespace Launches MiCA-Compliant Self-Custodial Bitcoin Debit Card Powered by Lightning and NWC
Wavespace, the Bitcoin-only neobank, has achieved MiCA compliance and launched its self-custodial wavecard® that uses Nostr Wallet Connect to automatically top up from users’ Lightning nodes, eliminating custodial preloading risks.
This post Wavespace Launches MiCA-Compliant Self-Custodial Bitcoin Debit Card Powered by Lightning and NWC first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Juan Galt.

title: Bitget Bolsters Stock+ Platform With U.S. Stock Options Trading
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitget Bolsters Stock+ Platform With U.S. Stock Options Trading
Bitget has expanded its Stock+ platform by launching U.S. stock options trading, adding long call and put options to its lineup as part of its push to combine crypto and traditional financial markets in a single trading platform.
This post Bitget Bolsters Stock+ Platform With U.S. Stock Options Trading first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

title: Metaplanet Adds 2,823 Bitcoin, Reaches 43,000 BTC and Becomes World’s Third-Largest Corporate Treasury
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Metaplanet added 2,823 BTC in the second quarter to reach 43,000 Bitcoin, becoming the world's third-largest corporate Bitcoin treasury behind Strategy and Twenty One Capital.
This post Metaplanet Adds 2,823 Bitcoin, Reaches 43,000 BTC and Becomes World’s Third-Largest Corporate Treasury first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

title: OpenAI floats giving Trump administration 5 percent cut of AI boom
content:
OpenAI has floated giving the US government a 5 percent ownership stake as a way of easing tensions with the Trump administration and blunting mounting public backlash against AI, according to the Financial Times.
CEO Sam Altman argued that giving the public a financial interest in the company would be the best way to share the upside of AI, the FT reported, citing two unnamed people familiar with the talks. He's said to have first pitched the idea to Trump early last year.
Altman reportedly suggested the 5 percent figure. Based on OpenAI's latest funding round, which ended with the company valued at $852 billion, that stake would be worth …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Google built a great smart speaker, but Gemini isn’t ready for it
content:
The Google Home Speaker is Google’s first smart speaker in years. And it’s pretty! | Photo: Jennifer Patison Tuohy / The Verge	

Smart speakers have spent the past few years searching for a compelling second act. Beyond music, timers, and controlling your lights, they've struggled to justify taking up space on the kitchen counter. AI promised to change that.
Amazon debuted its new hardware powered by a revamped Alexa last fall, and now it's finally Google's turn. The Google Home Speaker is the company's first new smart speaker in six years and its first "built for Gemini." After years of neglect, Google appears to be finally getting serious about the smart home - again. The new speaker is the clearest sign yet. Gemini for Home, however, still feels unfinished.
Google …

Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Netflix is using an AI-generated Gene Wilder voice in its Willy Wonka reality show
content:
A new teaser trailer confirmed that Wonka's The Golden Ticket will premiere on Netflix on September 23rd, following its Squid Game reality show in the trend of creating real competitions based on fictional torture scenarios. While the sets seen in the trailer are real and not some Glasgow-style AI fakes, the voiceover is AI-generated. 
Deadline reports that Netflix worked with AI audio company ElevenLabs with consent from Wilder's family, after working on productions re-creating the voice of Michael Caine and Stan Lee. It also continues Netflix's 2021 partnership with the Roald Dahl company, and is separate from the Charlie vs. the Chocolat …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Libby will filter out AI content, kind of
content:
This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week.
"AI is the new frontier for us," says Marc DeBevoise, who took over as the new CEO of OverDrive last week. OverDrive is best known for the ebook lending app Libby that is available through tens of thousands of public libraries. Like the rest of the digital publishing industry, it's poised to face massive disruption from a huge wave of AI-generated books.
To prepare for the AI onslaught, Libby is now getting ready to introduce AI content controls, allowing readers to select in t …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Meet the lawyer who beat Elon Musk — twice
content:
“After college, my plan was to come to New York and get a record deal.” | Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images	

Watching Elon Musk fulminate at Bill Savitt during Musk v. Altman - the case in which Musk sued Sam Altman and OpenAI instead of seeing a therapist about his AI failures - was a bit like watching a toddler have a temper tantrum at his nursery school teacher. Savitt's questions were "designed to trick me," Musk said. He also told Savitt at one point, "You mostly do unfair questions."
Savitt, who has the approximate demeanor of a handsome Droopy Dog, gently told Musk, "I am trying to put the questions as fairly as I can. I am doing my best." 
I've seen a number of styles of cross-examination. Savitt's was mild-mannered and soft-spoken; his q …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: OpenAI is teasing new hardware… for Codex
content:
OpenAI is releasing some sort of device related to its AI-powered coding tool, Codex, on July 15th. In a video posted to X on Monday, OpenAI shows a square-shaped device with several buttons, alongside the caption, "Your favorite Codex shortcuts are getting an upgrade."
This isn't the mysterious AI-powered device OpenAI is working on with former Apple designer Jony Ive, however. As shown in the teaser, OpenAI is launching the device in partnership with Work Louder, a company that sells an array of mechanical keyboards and macro pads with mappable keys, dials, and switches.
The silhouette of the device shown by OpenAI looks a bit like Work  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Lawmakers want to ban AI companies from selling your health data
content:
A new proposal would ban the sale of Americans' health and location information to data brokers - including information people reveal to an AI chatbot like ChatGPT or Claude. 
In the coming weeks, Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) and Representative Mary Gay Scanlon (D-PA) are planning to debut a new version of the Health and Location Data Protection Act that's better suited to the AI era. The former version of the bill, first introduced in June 2022, prohibited data brokers from collecting and selling health and location data. Four years later, it's expanded to ban other companies from selling such data to brokers, and to specifically cover  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: Anthropic says Alibaba must be punished for largest Claude cloning attack
content: Alibaba allegedly used 25,000 accounts to mine Claude over 28.8 million exchanges.

title: OpenAI and Broadcom announce chip designed for LLM inference at scale
content: The silicon race is heating up amid the struggle to keep up with demand.

title: Oracle’s 21,000 layoffs help drive its debt-fueled AI investments

title: GM installs robots at flagship EV factory after laying off 1,300 workers

title: How Anthropic may have talked itself into an AI export ban
content: The company warned about dangers of advanced AI far more than rival OpenAI.

title: Why California’s carbon manure math doesn’t add up
content: Something stinks in California’s climate policies. Years ago, the state set up a system that pays cattle farmers across the country to turn the methane emitted from cattle manure into natural gas, encouraging the dairy sector to produce a gas we burn instead of one that just pollutes the air. It’s become wildly popular because…
summary: California's climate policy offers payments to cattle farmers nationwide for converting methane from manure into natural gas. This popular system aims to reduce air pollution by burning the gas, but the article questions the effectiveness and logic of this 'carbon manure math'.

title: Achieving operational excellence with AI
content: Frameworks like Lean Six Sigma and business process management (BPM) first gained traction because they promised clarity in the chaos—a structured way to bring order to messy, sprawling operations. Lean Six Sigma emphasized statistical rigor and quality control; BPM created end-to-end maps of how work should flow across departments. Both offered a repeatable way to…
summary: This article introduces traditional operational excellence frameworks such as Lean Six Sigma and Business Process Management (BPM). These methodologies aim to bring structure and order to business operations by emphasizing statistical rigor, quality control, and mapping work flows. Although the title suggests a focus on AI, the provided content only describes these established business improvement approaches.
